selfHealing.heading: Self-Healing Test Flows
selfHealing.subheading: When your code changes, SimUser AI adapts automatically. No broken tests, no manual re-configuration.

example.heading: Real-World Example
example.description: Your team renames a button from 'Submit' to 'Send' — traditional E2E tests break instantly. SimUser's AI recognizes the intent hasn't changed and adapts the flow automatically. No engineer intervention required.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subheading: Everything you need to know about SimUser AI.

q1.question: How is SimUser AI different from traditional E2E testing tools?
q1.answer: E2E testing tools (Playwright, Cypress) verify whether features work — 'does the button click?'. SimUser AI evaluates whether features deliver a good experience — 'would a first-time user understand this flow?'. We complement E2E testing, not replace it.

q2.question: How accurate are the AI personas?
q2.answer: Our personas are grounded in actual browser interaction, not hypothetical feedback. Each persona carries behavioral profiles (patience levels, tech savviness, goals, frustration triggers) that influence how they navigate your app. Academic research shows simulated user feedback covers 70% of scenarios explored by human participants and identifies 80% of usability issues.

q3.question: How long does it take to set up?
q3.answer: Minutes, not weeks. Just provide your application URL and optional credentials. SimUser AI's auto-discovery engine maps your application's flows automatically — no SDK integration, no code changes, no test scripts to write.

q4.question: What happens when my UI changes?
q4.answer: SimUser AI's self-healing engine automatically detects code changes, re-discovers affected flows, and adapts test definitions — eliminating the 60-80% maintenance burden that plagues traditional test automation.

q5.question: Is my data secure?
q5.answer: Absolutely. We use AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, and store credentials in AWS Secrets Manager. Your data is never used to train AI models, and AWS Bedrock ensures model providers have zero access to your prompts. We're on the path to SOC 2 Type II certification.

q6.question: What does a credit include?
q6.answer: Each credit runs one AI persona through one flow (7-10 steps). That includes screenshots at every step, full video recording of the session, and a complete experience report with NPS score, qualitative feedback, and bug detection. Running 5 personas through 1 flow costs 5 credits.

q7.question: Can I integrate SimUser AI with my existing CI/CD pipeline?
q7.answer: Yes! We support GitHub Actions out of the box, with GitLab CI, Bitbucket Pipelines, and CircleCI coming soon. Trigger persona tests automatically on every deployment and get results directly in your workflow.

q8.question: Do I need to write any code?
q8.answer: No. SimUser AI automatically discovers your application's flows, runs AI personas through them, and generates experience reports — all without writing a single line of test code. You can also create custom personas and manually define flows through our web dashboard.

product.title: How SimUser AI Works

repoIntegration.heading: Connect Your Repository

repoIntegration.step1: Connect your GitHub or GitLab repository
repoIntegration.step2: SimUser AI detects new commits and PRs
repoIntegration.step4: Results posted as PR comments and Slack alerts
repoIntegration.caption: Seamless CI/CD integration — test every deploy with zero manual effort.

discovery.heading: Auto Flow Discovery
discovery.subheading: Provide your app URL and credentials. SimUser AI's discovery engine automatically maps your application's flows.
discovery.step3: Identifies navigation paths and interactive elements
discovery.step4: Constructs structured flow maps (7-10 steps each)
discovery.step5: Users can modify or create flows manually
discovery.caption: Each flow represents a meaningful user journey — Sign Up, Onboarding, Checkout, etc.

dashboard.heading: Dashboard & Flow Management
dashboard.subheading: A central command center to manage your flows, personas, and test results in one place.
dashboard.flowDiagramsDesc: Interactive visual representations of every discovered user flow, with step-by-step navigation paths.
dashboard.manualFlowsDesc: Create and customize flows manually when you need precise control over specific user journeys.
dashboard.manualPersonasDesc: Configure persona profiles, assign them to flows, and fine-tune behavioral parameters.
dashboard.liveStatusDesc: Real-time monitoring of active test sessions with live progress updates and preliminary results.

personas.heading: AI Persona Engine
personas.subheading: 7 built-in personas with complete behavioral profiles. Each carries patience levels, tech savviness, goals, and frustration triggers.
personas.customLabel: Create custom personas for your audience

execution.heading: Execution Engine
execution.subheading: Agentic loop with up to 30 turns per session. Each persona navigates your app with realistic human behavior patterns.
execution.toolsDesc: Navigation, clicking, form input, file upload, scrolling, waiting, screenshot capture, network interception, and assertion validation.
execution.screenshotsDesc: Every interaction step is captured as a screenshot for visual evidence.
execution.videoDesc: Complete session video showing exactly how the persona navigated your app.
execution.actionsDesc: Detailed logs of every decision point and observation the persona made.

reports.heading: Report Engine
reports.subheading: Comprehensive experience reports that transform raw interactions into actionable product intelligence.
reports.npsDesc: Reflects the persona's overall experience based on behavioral profile and friction encountered.
reports.feedbackDesc: User-voice observations, not technical QA language — 'I couldn't figure out how to get back to the dashboard.'
reports.bugsDesc: Functional issues, broken links, error states, and unexpected behaviors encountered during the session.
reports.confusionDesc: Moments where the persona hesitated, backtracked, or failed to find the expected path.
reports.gradeDesc: Breakdown across NPS, goal completion, bugs found, and confusion points.
reports.consolidatedDesc: Multi-persona synthesis: '3 of 5 personas failed to complete checkout at the same payment form step.'

selfHealing.heading: Self-Healing Architecture
selfHealing.subheading: When your code changes, SimUser AI adapts automatically. No broken tests, no manual re-configuration.
selfHealing.step1Desc: Detects that previously mapped flows no longer match the current application state.
selfHealing.step2Desc: AI agent re-explores affected areas, identifying new elements, modified paths, and changed interactions.
selfHealing.step3Desc: Existing flow definitions are updated. New steps added, removed steps pruned, modified interactions adjusted.
selfHealing.step4Desc: Persona sessions re-run against updated flows, ensuring feedback reflects the current product state.

comparison.heading: How We Compare
comparison.subheading: SimUser AI vs traditional and AI testing approaches.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Each credit represents one persona executing one flow through your application. A flow consists of 7-10 steps that the AI persona navigates, including screenshots, video recording, and a full experience report with NPS score. Running 5 personas through 1 flow = 5 credits.
faq.q2: How many credits do I need?
faq.a2: It depends on your testing needs. A typical startup might run 5-10 flows with 5 personas each, using 25-50 credits per testing cycle. The Starter plan (50 credits/month) covers most early-stage needs.
faq.a3: SimUser AI uses compute-intensive resources for each test (LLM inference, browser automation, video recording, storage). Free tiers in AI products typically lead to 95-98% non-converting users who represent pure cost. Instead, we offer a 7-day free trial with 10 credits — enough to experience the full value before committing.
faq.q4: How is this different from E2E testing?
faq.a4: E2E testing tools (Playwright, Cypress) verify whether features work — 'does the button click?'. SimUser AI evaluates whether features deliver a good experience — 'would a first-time user understand this flow?'. We complement E2E testing, not replace it.
faq.a5: Yes! Beyond the 7 built-in personas, you can create custom personas tailored to your specific product and audience. Define tech savviness, patience level, goals, and frustration triggers to match your actual users.
faq.a6: SimUser AI uses Playwright for browser automation, supporting Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit — covering approximately 95% of global browser usage. Mobile device emulation is also available with 30+ device descriptors.
faq.q7: Do you store my application credentials?
faq.a7: Credentials are encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3), stored in AWS Secrets Manager, and never logged or transmitted to LLM inference endpoints. They are only used during test execution.
faq.q8: What happens when my app changes?
faq.a8: SimUser AI's self-healing engine automatically detects code changes, re-discovers affected flows, and adapts test definitions — eliminating the 60-80% maintenance burden that plagues traditional test automation.

security.title: Enterprise-Grade Security
security.subtitle: Your data security is our top priority. SimUser AI implements privacy-preserving practices at every layer.

layers.heading: Architecture Security Layers
layers.subheading: Defense in depth — five layers protecting your data at every level.

waf.title: WAF & Rate Limiting
waf.description: Web Application Firewall and intelligent rate limiting protect against DDoS, brute force, and abuse.

network.title: Network Isolation
network.description: VPC-based network isolation with private subnets, security groups, and no public-facing database endpoints.

encryption.title: Credential Encryption
encryption.description: AES-256 encryption at rest via AWS Secrets Manager. TLS 1.3 in transit for all data. Credentials never logged or sent to LLM endpoints.

database.title: Database Isolation
database.description: Per-tenant data isolation with dedicated encryption keys. No cross-customer data mixing in LLM calls or storage.

audit.title: Audit Logging
audit.description: Comprehensive audit trail for all data access, credential usage, and administrative actions.

aiCommitments.heading: AI/LLM Security Commitments
aiCommitments.subheading: What we promise about how we use AI foundation models with your data.

noTraining.title: No Training on Customer Data
noTraining.description: Your application data, screenshots, and test results are never used to train any AI model.

noAccess.title: Zero Model Provider Access
noAccess.description: AWS Bedrock ensures model providers (Anthropic) have zero access to your prompts and completions.

iso.title: ISO/IEC 42001
iso.description: AWS Bedrock holds ISO/IEC 42001 certification — the international standard for AI management systems.

privateLink.title: Private Connectivity
privateLink.description: AWS PrivateLink ensures LLM API calls never traverse the public internet.

guardrails.title: AI Guardrails & PII Masking
guardrails.description: Automatic PII detection and masking in screenshots before any data is sent to LLM inference endpoints.

compliance.heading: Compliance Roadmap
compliance.subheading: Our path to comprehensive compliance coverage.

gdpr.title: GDPR
gdpr.description: DPIA templates, SCCs for cross-border transfers, Article 25 data protection by design.

soc2.title: SOC 2 Type II
soc2.description: SOC 2 Type II certification planned for Q4 2026, covering security, availability, and confidentiality.

lgpd.title: LGPD
lgpd.description: Data subject requests within 15 days, breach notification within 72 hours, ANPD-approved SCCs.

iso27001.title: ISO 27001
iso27001.description: Information security management system certification planned for 2028.

dataProtection.heading: Data Protection by Design
dataProtection.subheading: Privacy is built into every layer of SimUser AI, not bolted on.

pii.title: PII Detection & Masking
pii.description: Screenshots are processed to detect and redact PII (names, emails, addresses, phone numbers, financial data) before storage or LLM transmission.

credentials.title: Credential Isolation
credentials.description: AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit. Stored in AWS Secrets Manager, never logged or transmitted to LLM endpoints.

retention.title: Configurable Retention
retention.description: Default 30-day retention with customizable policies. Customers can set shorter retention or trigger immediate deletion.

isolation.title: Per-Tenant Data Isolation
isolation.description: Each customer's data is isolated — no cross-customer data mixing in LLM calls, no shared storage buckets, no aggregated training.

founderCta.heading: Have security questions?
founderCta.message: Security is core to our team after years of leading SRE and Security teams. We're happy to walk through our security architecture in detail.

finalCta.title: Trust SimUser AI with your application
finalCta.subtitle: Enterprise-grade security from day one. Join the waitlist for early access.

about.title: About SimUser AI
about.subtitle: We believe every product deserves to be tested by the users it's built for — even when those users don't exist yet.

mission.heading: Our Mission
mission.statement: Every product deserves to be tested by the users it's built for — even when those users don't exist yet.
mission.description: The world is building software faster than ever — 41% of code is now AI-generated. But code quality is declining proportionally. The gap between development velocity and user experience quality is widening. SimUser AI exists to close that gap: making professional-quality user experience validation accessible to every software team.

team.heading: The Team
team.subheading: Built by engineers who lived the problem.

ceo.relevance: Deep understanding of software quality challenges, testing workflows, and the pain points SimUser AI addresses.

cto.name: AI/ML Lead
cto.relevance: Builds the autonomous persona engine and agentic architecture that powers SimUser AI.

academic.heading: Academic Validation
academic.subheading: Our approach is grounded in peer-reviewed research.

paper3.title: SimUser: Generating Usability Feedback
paper3.description: 20 rounds of simulated feedback covered 70% of scenarios explored by 48 human participants and identified 80% of usability issues.

compare.heading: Why Teams Choose SimUser AI
compare.subtitle: Every alternative leaves a gap. SimUser AI fills them all.

momentic.name: AI E2E Test Tool
momentic.keyWeakness: Verifies that buttons work, but has zero insight into whether users actually enjoy the experience. No persona simulation, no qualitative feedback, no NPS.
momentic.simuserAdvantage: SimUser AI tells you what users think and feel about your product — not just whether the code executes.

qaWolf.name: Managed QA Service
qaWolf.keyWeakness: Charges $15-20K+/month, requires a 4-month onboarding, and delivers test results — not user experience insights. Built for large enterprise engineering teams only.
qaWolf.simuserAdvantage: SimUser AI delivers persona-driven UX feedback in minutes, starting at $49/month. No onboarding. No human QA team required.

applause.name: Crowdsourced Testing Platform
applause.keyWeakness: Coordinates 300,000 human testers at $25K-$590K/year. Results take weeks. Tester quality varies. Scheduling is a nightmare.
applause.simuserAdvantage: SimUser AI replaces weeks of coordination with AI personas that deliver consistent, reproducible results in minutes — at 99% less cost.

syntheticUsers.name: AI Interview Tool
syntheticUsers.keyWeakness: Runs text-only interviews about what users might do. No browser. No screenshots. No video. No evidence of real interaction with your product.
syntheticUsers.simuserAdvantage: SimUser AI personas open real browsers and interact with your live app — delivering screenshots, video recordings, and observed behavior, not hypotheticals.

blok.name: Synthetic A/B Platform
blok.keyWeakness: Validates product concepts before you build them. Cannot test your live application. No real browser execution, no session recordings, no NPS on actual usage.
blok.simuserAdvantage: SimUser AI tests your shipped product in real browsers — every click is real, every NPS score is grounded in actual interaction.

uxia.name: Design File Analyzer
uxia.keyWeakness: Analyzes static Figma/Sketch mockups for heatmaps and WCAG compliance. Cannot test live applications, discover broken flows, or give qualitative persona feedback.
uxia.simuserAdvantage: SimUser AI tests your deployed application with 7 AI personas that navigate, click, and report back — with full session recordings and NPS scores.

octomind.name: AI Test Generator
octomind.keyWeakness: Auto-generates Playwright tests and tells you if they pass or fail. Has no concept of user experience — no personas, no qualitative feedback, no NPS. Starts at $89/month.
octomind.simuserAdvantage: SimUser AI goes beyond pass/fail. Get NPS scores, qualitative feedback from 7 personas, and experience reports — starting at $49/month.

cta.title: Ready to see the difference?
cta.subtitle: Join the waitlist and start testing with AI personas that think like real users.
